text:
# Rocky — IIM Bangalore Marketing Strategy RAG

This pack is a source-labelled, original synthesis of publicly accessible IIM Bangalore/IIMBx marketing material for Rocky. It is not an official IIMB knowledge base and contains no private course notes or textbook copying.

Keep this collection separate from the current Meta/Google platform-mechanics collection. Retrieve both when Rocky creates campaigns.

Ingest `rag_chunks.jsonl`; embed `text` and retain the remaining fields as metadata.

# Marketing ROI and Media Metrics
Sources:
- https://www.iimb.ac.in/programmes/pgpem/structure-curriculum
- https://www.iimb.ac.in/node/14039
Knowledge version: 2026-07

IIMB programme material explicitly includes Marketing ROI & Media Metrics and Marketing Analytics. IIMB's 2025 Marketing Management book-launch summary highlights CLV, segmentation, positioning models and marketing ROI metrics.

## Rocky measurement hierarchy
Business outcomes: revenue, gross/contribution value where available, qualified pipeline, customer acquisition, retention/LTV.
Conversion outcomes: purchases, qualified leads, booked appointments, applications, high-value actions.
Media outcomes: spend, impressions, reach, clicks, video/engagement metrics.
Diagnostic outcomes: CTR, CPC, CPM, frequency, landing-page behaviour and funnel-stage rates.

Rocky must not optimize a business purely around vanity metrics. A cheap click is not automatically a valuable customer.

ROAS alone can be insufficient when margins, repeat purchase, sales-cycle length or lead quality materially affect economics.

# Customer Value, CRM and Lifetime Thinking
Sources:
- https://www.iimb.ac.in/programmes/pgpem/structure-curriculum
- https://www.iimb.ac.in/node/14039
Knowledge version: 2026-07

IIMB marketing curricula include Customer Relationship Management, pricing, analytics and ROI. Current IIMB material also highlights customer lifetime value.

## Rocky implications
Where data exists, campaign evaluation should consider:
- first-order/customer acquisition value,
- lead-to-sale rate,
- average order/deal value,
- gross margin,
- repeat purchase/retention,
- LTV,
- payback period,
- sales-cycle lag.

For lead generation, feed qualified-lead/sale outcomes back into analysis when technically possible. Do not equate raw lead count with business success.

# IIMB Digital Marketing Principles
Source: https://www.iimb.ac.in/node/5597
Knowledge version: 2026-07

IIM Bangalore's publication record for Prof. Seema Gupta's Digital Marketing describes an approach blending theory and practice, platform selection according to customer needs and behavioural patterns, user-experience optimization, campaign optimization, SEO, mobile/e-marketing, cases and real-world examples.

## Rocky strategy rules
- Start with the customer and business problem, not the ad platform.
- Select channels according to customer behaviour and campaign purpose.
- Treat campaign execution and optimization as part of one feedback loop.
- Landing/user experience affects whether paid-media demand becomes business value.
- Platform mechanics should be retrieved from Rocky's separate current Meta/Google official-docs RAG, because platform interfaces and rules change faster than durable marketing principles.

# IIM Bangalore-Derived Marketing Framework
Source basis:
- https://www.iimb.ac.in/faculty/marketing
- https://www.iimb.ac.in/programmes/pgpem/structure-curriculum
Knowledge version: 2026-07

IIM Bangalore's Marketing area frames marketing around creating, communicating, capturing and sustaining value. Its current programme listings include Digital Marketing Strategies, Consumer Behaviour, Marketing Analytics, Marketing Communication, Marketing ROI & Media Metrics, CRM, Brand Management, Competitive Marketing Strategy, Research for Marketing Decisions, pricing and related subjects.

## Rocky interpretation
Paid advertising is not an isolated button-pushing activity. Rocky should connect campaign choices to:
1. customer value,
2. target segment,
3. positioning,
4. communication,
5. acquisition economics,
6. relationship/retention,
7. measurable business outcomes.

This file is an original synthesis for Rocky, not IIMB course text.

# Source Integrity Rules
Knowledge version: 2026-07

Labels:
IIMB_PUBLIC_SOURCE = facts directly supported by publicly accessible IIM Bangalore/IIMBx pages.
IIMB_DERIVED_SYNTHESIS = original Rocky strategy inference based on those public themes.
META_OFFICIAL_RULE = retrieve from separate current Meta official knowledge base.
ACCOUNT_DATA = connected Skyup advertising/account data.
CLIENT_DATA = must remain isolated according to Rocky's data architecture.

Never label authored Rocky advice as an IIM Bangalore recommendation.
Never reproduce paywalled/private course notes, copyrighted textbooks or classroom materials into the vector database without appropriate rights.
Never infer that IIMB endorses Rocky, Skyup or a campaign.

# Rocky Bridge — IIMB Strategy + Meta Ads Mechanics
Knowledge version: 2026-07

This collection does NOT claim to be an official "IIM Bangalore Meta Ads course database."

Use two knowledge layers:
1. IIMB-derived strategy knowledge: customer behaviour, segmentation, positioning, marketing analytics, ROI, communication and research logic.
2. Rocky's official Meta Ads RAG: current objectives, audience mechanics, placements, bidding, Pixel/CAPI, policies and platform constraints.

## Retrieval sequence for a Meta campaign
A. Retrieve customer/segment/positioning knowledge from this IIMB-derived collection.
B. Analyze business economics and funnel.
C. Form campaign hypotheses.
D. Retrieve current Meta platform mechanics from official Meta collection.
E. Translate strategy into objective, optimization, audience controls/signals, placements, creative requirements and measurement.
F. Validate policy/privacy.
G. Produce plan with explicit assumptions and missing data.

This prevents old academic/platform examples from overriding current Meta mechanics.

# Positioning-to-Campaign Translation
Grounded in:
- https://iimbx.iimb.ac.in/catalog/consumer-behaviour/
- https://www.iimb.ac.in/programmes/pgpem/structure-curriculum
Knowledge version: 2026-07

## Decision chain
Market → segment → customer need → positioning → offer → message → channel → campaign objective → audience/keywords → creative → landing experience → conversion → retention.

Rocky should reject plans where these layers contradict each other.

Examples:
- Premium positioning + discount-only creative may erode intended positioning.
- High-consideration service + instant-purchase assumptions may misread the buying journey.
- Awareness problem + aggressive last-click-only evaluation can undervalue upper-funnel activity.
- Lead-volume optimization + no lead-quality feedback can optimize toward low-value leads.

Use the official Meta/Google RAG for exact platform settings.

# Campaign Hypothesis Engine
Grounded in IIMB themes of research for marketing decisions, analytical marketing, consumer behaviour, digital marketing and marketing ROI.
Source: https://www.iimb.ac.in/programmes/pgpem/structure-curriculum
Knowledge version: 2026-07

Every meaningful campaign decision should be expressible as a hypothesis.

Template:
Because [customer/market evidence], we believe [segment] will respond to [offer/message] on [channel/context], producing [measurable outcome]. We will evaluate using [metric] over [appropriate test window], while monitoring [guardrail metrics].

Rocky should separate:
- fact,
- user/account data,
- official platform rule,
- strategic inference,
- experiment hypothesis.

Do not disguise a hypothesis as established fact.

# IIMB Consumer Behaviour — Segmentation and Positioning
Source: https://iimbx.iimb.ac.in/catalog/consumer-behaviour/
Knowledge version: 2026-07

IIMBx's Consumer Behaviour syllabus covers segmentation, brand positioning, targeting, marketing-mix elements, psychographics, brand value and consumer profiling.

## Rocky application
Before defining a Meta audience or Google keyword universe, establish:
- who the meaningful customer segment is,
- the customer's job/problem/desire,
- relevant demographic/geographic constraints,
- psychographic or behavioural distinctions where evidence exists,
- the value proposition,
- competitive frame,
- desired positioning.

Platform targeting is an implementation of market segmentation; it is not a substitute for segmentation.

Avoid fake personas. If evidence is missing, label persona assumptions as hypotheses to test.

# IIMB Consumer Behaviour — Decision Psychology
Sources:
- https://iimbx.iimb.ac.in/catalog/consumer-behaviour/
- https://research.iimb.ac.in/books/10/
Knowledge version: 2026-07

IIMB material covers consumer decision making, cultural influences, experiential utility, motivation, emotion, perception, learning, conditioning, psychographics and consumer profiling. Prof. Seema Gupta's IIMB-listed work on online buying focuses on consumer psychology and behavioural economics in digital commerce.

## Rocky application to ads
Creative strategy should identify:
- customer motivation,
- perceived risk,
- desired outcome,
- decision friction,
- existing beliefs,
- trust evidence,
- emotional and rational considerations,
- stage of awareness.

Do not use psychological concepts to make unsupported claims about an individual. Use them as segment-level creative hypotheses.

For high-consideration purchases, Rocky should distinguish attention, evaluation, trust formation and conversion rather than assuming one ad exposure causes purchase.

# IIMB Consumer Behaviour — Perception and Learning
Source: https://iimbx.iimb.ac.in/catalog/consumer-behaviour/
Knowledge version: 2026-07

IIMBx explicitly includes perception, thresholds, learning, classical conditioning and practical applications.

## Rocky creative implications
Consistency of brand cues can support recognition and learning. Creative variation should test messages without destroying essential brand recognition. Ads must make the key value proposition perceptible quickly enough for the placement context.

Rocky should formulate testable creative hypotheses such as:
"Showing proof earlier may reduce perceived risk."
It must not present behavioural theory as a guaranteed conversion hack.

# Rocky 3-Level Execution RAG v1

Use `rag_chunks.jsonl` for ingestion. Embed `text` and retain the remaining fields as metadata.

Coverage:
- Meta: campaign → ad set → ad
- Google: campaign → ad group/asset group → ad/assets
- measurement + India operational note
- Rocky execution/retrieval/anti-hallucination rules

This is the first execution-depth pack, not a mirror of every help page. Exact live IDs, eligibility, API enums, forecasts and account capabilities must come from the connected platforms.

# Rocky Advertising Anti-Hallucination Rules
Platform: cross_platform
Level: system
Topic: quality

Never invent audience size, interest IDs, keyword volume, CPC, CPM, CPA, CPL, conversion rate, ROAS, competitor spend, platform forecasts, customer demographics, testimonials, offers, prices, credentials, tracking status or expected results.

Static RAG cannot prove live eligibility, inventory, account restrictions, API support or performance.

# Rocky Executable Campaign Contract
Platform: cross_platform
Level: system
Topic: execution

Every plan must distinguish VERIFIED_ACCOUNT_DATA, USER_PROVIDED_DATA, OFFICIAL_PLATFORM_RULE, STRATEGY_INFERENCE and MISSING_DATA.

Before API creation, validate every required field, enum and platform ID against the connected API/account. RAG explains rules; live API capability determines what can actually be submitted. Default new campaigns to PAUSED for human review.

# Rocky Three-Level Retrieval Plan
Platform: cross_platform
Level: system
Topic: retrieval

Meta retrieval: campaign/objective + ad-set optimization + audience/placements + ad/creative + measurement + execution rules.
Google retrieval: campaign type/bidding + ad-group or asset-group knowledge + ad/assets + measurement + execution rules.

Use metadata filters for platform + level + topic before semantic ranking. Do not use one generic top-k query for the entire campaign plan.

# Google Ads India Tax Operational Note
Platform: google_ads
Level: account
Topic: tax
Country: IN
Source: https://support.google.com/google-ads/answer/2375370?hl=en
Verified: 2026-07-24

Google's current India documentation lists CGST 9% + SGST 9% for applicable intra-state treatment and IGST 18% for applicable inter-state treatment, with documented exceptions including eligible SEZ treatment.

This is an operational note, not tax advice.

# Google Measurement — 2026
Platform: google_ads
Level: measurement
Topic: enhanced_conversions
Sources:
- https://support.google.com/google-ads/answer/14664077?hl=en
- https://support.google.com/google-ads/answer/10000067?hl=en
Verified: 2026-07-24

Google documents unified enhanced-conversion changes in 2026, accepting user-provided data from website tags, Data Manager and API connections. Consent Mode communicates consent choices and changes supported tag behavior; it is not itself a consent banner.

Rocky must verify measurement state rather than infer it from campaign configuration.

# Google Performance Max
Platform: google_ads
Level: campaign
Topic: performance_max
Sources:
- https://support.google.com/google-ads/answer/14530785
- https://support.google.com/google-ads/answer/16669486?hl=en
Verified: 2026-07-24

Performance Max is goal-based. Audience signals are suggestions that guide Google AI, not exclusive hard targeting. Search themes provide words and phrases describing likely customer searches and additional business context; they are not traditional Search keywords.

Rocky must never represent audience signals or search themes as deterministic targeting.

# Google Bidding and Budget Guardrails
Platform: google_ads
Level: campaign
Topic: bidding_budget

Select bidding according to the optimization goal and quality of conversion/value data. Never invent Target CPA or Target ROAS from generic benchmarks.

When reliable historical economics are unavailable, an initial approach may be proposed only as STRATEGY_INFERENCE. Connected account history and live forecasts take precedence over generic assumptions.

# Google Campaign Type Selection
Platform: google_ads
Level: campaign
Topic: campaign_type
Source: https://support.google.com/google-ads/answer/2567043?hl=en
Verified: 2026-07-24

Google campaign selection starts from goals and campaign type. Search captures active search demand. Performance Max is goal-based and can access inventory across Google channels using Smart Bidding. Other types serve different inventory and objectives.

Rocky decision order: business outcome → conversion goal → demand state → inventory/creative fit → campaign type. Do not choose PMax merely because it spans channels, or Search when the core job is primarily visual demand creation.

# Google Search Ad Group Structure
Platform: google_ads
Level: ad_group
Topic: search_structure
Source: https://developers.google.com/google-ads/api/fields/v22/ad_group
Verified: 2026-07-24

Google Ads ad groups expose bidding, settings, status, relationships and performance data. Rocky should group search intent coherently so ads and landing pages match the underlying intent.

Avoid excessive micro-groups without a relevance, reporting or testing reason.

# Google Keywords and Negative Keywords
Platform: google_ads
Level: ad_group
Topic: keywords
Source: https://support.google.com/google-ads/answer/2453972
Verified: 2026-07-24

Negative keywords exclude unwanted search intent. Google documents broad, phrase and exact negative matching and notes that negative matching differs from positive matching.

Rocky must derive keyword themes from the real offer and search intent, separate materially different intent, and exclude clearly irrelevant meanings. Never invent search volume, CPC, competition or forecasts; use live Keyword Planner/account data for numerical evidence.

# Google Performance Max Asset Groups
Platform: google_ads
Level: asset_group
Topic: pmax_assets
Sources:
- https://support.google.com/google-ads/answer/17091269?hl=en
- https://support.google.com/google-ads/answer/14530785
Verified: 2026-07-24

Asset groups combine creative inputs and can include audience signals. Signals guide machine learning but do not constrain delivery exclusively to those users.

Build asset groups around coherent business/creative themes. Avoid nearly identical duplication without a measurement reason.

# Performance Max Search Themes
Platform: google_ads
Level: asset_group
Topic: search_themes
Source: https://support.google.com/google-ads/answer/16669486?hl=en
Verified: 2026-07-24

Search themes communicate business and customer search context to Performance Max. They differ from traditional keywords and act as AI guidance.

Use them for useful context that landing pages, feeds or assets may not communicate clearly. Do not treat them as exact Search targeting.

# Google Responsive Search Ads
Platform: google_ads
Level: ad
Topic: responsive_search_ad
Source: https://developers.google.com/google-ads/api/docs/responsive-search-ads/get-responsive-search-ads
Verified: 2026-07-24

Responsive Search Ads contain multiple headline and description assets that Google can combine. The API exposes RSA headlines and descriptions on the ad resource.

Create materially distinct, truthful assets tied to search intent. Never fabricate claims, prices or credentials. Validate exact current asset limits before submission.

# Performance Max Asset Specifications
Platform: google_ads
Level: ad
Topic: asset_specs
Source: https://support.google.com/google-ads/answer/17091269?hl=en
Verified: 2026-07-24

Google's current official PMax specification states headline assets allow up to 30 characters, with at least 3 and at most 15 headlines for the relevant non-feed asset-group requirement. Other asset types have separate requirements.

Rocky must retrieve current specifications before producing a final executable creative manifest. Never generalize one asset type's limits to another format.

# Meta Campaign Preflight
Platform: meta_ads
Level: campaign
Topic: preflight

Resolve before creation: business goal, platform objective, geography, conversion location, performance goal, budget ownership, schedule, destination, measurement readiness, creative availability, and applicable restricted/special-category requirements.

Unknown required values are MISSING_DATA. Never fabricate them. Rocky should create campaigns PAUSED for human review by default.

# Meta Campaign Objectives
Platform: meta_ads
Level: campaign
Topic: objective_selection
Source: https://www.facebook.com/business/ads/ad-objectives
Verified: 2026-07-24

Meta's auction uses the selected objective to pursue the result the advertiser wants. Rocky must map the real business outcome to the closest platform objective rather than choosing from industry alone.

Rules:
- Do not use Traffic when the real primary outcome is measurable leads or sales merely because a website is involved.
- Do not default every service business to Leads without resolving the actual conversion journey.
- Keep the business objective separate from the Meta objective.
- Validate objective, conversion location and performance-goal compatibility against the current platform before API creation.

# Meta Pixel and Conversions API
Platform: meta_ads
Level: ad
Topic: measurement
Source: https://www.facebook.com/business/help/AboutConversionsAPI
Verified: 2026-07-24

Conversions API connects business marketing data to Meta systems for optimization and measurement and supports website, app, offline and messaging event use cases. Meta recommends considering CAPI alongside Pixel for website events.

Rocky must verify event source, desired event, browser/server deduplication where relevant, and downstream signal quality. CAPI is not a privacy-policy bypass. Never claim Pixel/CAPI is healthy without connected evidence.

# Meta Copy, CTA and Destination
Platform: meta_ads
Level: ad
Topic: copy_destination

Maintain message match across hook, value proposition, proof, CTA and destination. The CTA must correspond to the intended action and the destination must exist.

For lead journeys, distinguish website, form, call and messaging flows where available. Do not fabricate character limits; retrieve current specifications for the exact format/placement.

# Meta Ad Creative Layer
Platform: meta_ads
Level: ad
Topic: creative

Choose creative according to objective, placement context, customer insight and assets that actually exist.

Never invent images, videos, testimonials, discounts, accreditation, customer counts, prices, guarantees or performance claims. Exact placement-specific dimensions, duration and text recommendations should be resolved from current Meta specifications for the selected format before final execution.

# Meta Advantage+ Placements
Platform: meta_ads
Level: ad_set
Topic: placements
Source: https://www.facebook.com/business/ads/meta-advantage-plus/placements
Verified: 2026-07-24

Advantage+ placements can distribute ads across eligible Meta surfaces including Facebook, Instagram, Reels, Stories, Messenger and Audience Network.

Automated placements are a reasonable performance default when compatible with the campaign. Restrict placements only for a concrete reason such as creative compatibility, a deliberate experiment, brand safety, legal constraints or destination limitations. Never claim a placement is universally best for Indian SMBs without account evidence.

# Meta Conversion Location and Performance Goal
Platform: meta_ads
Level: ad_set
Topic: optimization
Sources:
- https://www.facebook.com/business/ads/ad-objectives
- https://www.facebook.com/business/ads/ad-objectives/traffic
Verified: 2026-07-24

The objective alone does not fully define delivery. Rocky must reason about where the desired action occurs and what performance result the ad set should optimize toward.

The exact options exposed by Ads Manager/API are live platform capability. Validate objective → conversion location → performance goal before generating an executable payload.

# Meta Advantage+ Audience
Platform: meta_ads
Level: ad_set
Topic: audience
Source: https://www.facebook.com/business/ads/meta-advantage-plus/audience
Verified: 2026-07-24

Advantage+ audience can treat customer lists, lookalikes, age, gender and detailed targeting as suggestions and expand beyond them when Meta predicts improved performance. Meta documents stricter controls including location, minimum age, language and custom-audience exclusions.

Rocky must distinguish audience suggestions from controls. Never claim an interest suggestion guarantees exclusive delivery. Never invent audience size or Meta interest IDs. Human-readable interests require live platform lookup before execution.

# Meta Ad Set Testing
Platform: meta_ads
Level: ad_set
Topic: experimentation

A useful test changes a meaningful variable while preserving enough other conditions to interpret the result. Candidate dimensions include audience approach, offer/message, geography, optimization approach or placement strategy when supported by a real hypothesis.

Do not create many tiny ad sets merely to appear sophisticated. Do not label ordinary edits as controlled experiments.

# Rocky Ads RAG Knowledge Pack

Prepared: 2026-07-24

This package contains RAG-ready, original summaries grounded in publicly accessible official Google Ads and Meta documentation. It contains no Skyup client CRM/customer data and does not bulk-copy proprietary course content.

## Contents
- `google/` — campaign types, Search, bidding, PMax, Demand Gen, measurement, India/policy.
- `meta/` — objectives/structure, audiences, placements, budget/bidding/learning, Pixel/CAPI.
- `strategy/` — Rocky campaign decision and retrieval rules.
- `rag_chunks.jsonl` — pre-split records ready for embedding.
- `manifest.json` — source registry and pack metadata.

## Recommended ingestion
Embed the `text` field from each JSONL record and retain every other field as metadata. At retrieval time filter by platform/country/topic/path when possible. Never treat strategy inference as live account data.

## Refresh
Ad-platform behavior changes often. Revalidate official sources before major releases and refresh this pack on a schedule.

# Meta Ads — Budget, Bidding and Learning
Platform: meta_ads
Knowledge version: 2026-07
Sources:
- https://www.facebook.com/business/ads/pricing
- https://www.facebook.com/business/m/one-sheeters/facebook-bid-strategy-guide
- https://www.facebook.com/business/ads/performance-marketing
- https://www.facebook.com/help/messenger-app/650774041651557/

Meta supports campaign-budget and ad-set-budget approaches depending on campaign setup. Advantage+ campaign budget can distribute a campaign budget across ad sets dynamically.

Meta's bid-strategy guidance includes approaches oriented toward volume/value and controls such as ROAS floors or bid caps in eligible contexts. More restrictive controls can reduce delivery when the system cannot satisfy them.

During learning, delivery is exploring and optimizing; Meta states performance can be less stable and CPAs are usually worse. Significant edits can cause delivery to re-enter preparation/learning states.

## Rocky rules
- Avoid unnecessary structural fragmentation.
- Avoid frequent major edits merely because of short-term volatility.
- Do not invent a universal "perfect daily budget".
- Base budget on business economics, conversion expectations, test duration and actual account data.
- Advanced bid caps/ROAS controls should not be recommended casually without sufficient economics/history.

# Meta Ads — Pixel and Conversions API
Platform: meta_ads
Knowledge version: 2026-07
Sources:
- https://www.facebook.com/help/messenger-app/952192354843755/
- https://www.facebook.com/business/help/AboutConversionsAPI

The Meta Pixel is a browser-side business tool used to send website events. Meta recommends considering Conversions API alongside Pixel for website events.

Conversions API creates a direct connection between marketing data from sources such as servers, websites, apps, CRM/offline systems or messaging and Meta's systems for measurement and optimization. Meta states CAPI can improve connectivity and measurement and can be less affected by browser loading errors, connectivity issues and ad blockers than Pixel alone.

CAPI is not a mechanism to bypass privacy requirements or platform data-sharing rules.

## Rocky measurement gate
Check:
- Pixel/dataset status
- prioritized/primary business events
- event duplication/deduplication when browser + server events are used
- event match quality where available
- purchase values or qualified-lead signals
- CAPI availability
- consent/privacy requirements

Never claim CAPI or Pixel is healthy without evidence from Events Manager or integration diagnostics.

# Meta Ads — Campaign Structure and Objectives
Platform: meta_ads
Knowledge version: 2026-07
Source: https://www.facebook.com/help/messenger-app/621956575422138/

Meta Ads Manager uses campaign → ad set → ad structure. At the ad set level, advertisers commonly define audience, placements, budget/schedule depending on configuration. At the ad level, creative, format, copy, links and related assets are configured.

Current objective families surfaced by Meta include Awareness, Traffic, Engagement, Leads, App promotion and Sales.

## Rocky objective rules
- Awareness: prioritize reach/awareness outcomes when conversion is not the immediate primary goal.
- Traffic: use when the desired optimization is traffic/visits rather than pretending it is a sales optimizer.
- Engagement: use for engagement/messaging-oriented outcomes when appropriate.
- Leads: use when the actual outcome is lead capture/qualification.
- App promotion: use for app-focused outcomes.
- Sales: use when measurable purchase/sales outcomes are the primary goal.

Rocky must map business outcome to objective, not simply pick Leads for every service business or Sales for every ecommerce business without checking tracking and funnel readiness.

# Meta Ads — Advantage+ Audience
Platform: meta_ads
Knowledge version: 2026-07
Source: https://www.facebook.com/business/ads/meta-advantage-plus/audience

Advantage+ audience combines advertiser inputs with Meta's AI-driven delivery. Audience suggestions can include custom audiences, lookalikes, age, gender and detailed targeting. Meta may search beyond suggestions when it predicts better performance.

Meta documents audience controls that can remain strict for constraints such as location, minimum age, language and custom-audience exclusions, depending on setup.

## Rocky rules
- Distinguish audience controls from audience suggestions.
- Do not tell users that an interest suggestion guarantees delivery only to that interest.
- Use first-party/custom audiences only when the advertiser has lawful and platform-compliant rights to use the data.
- Avoid excessive fragmentation without a business reason.
- Do not invent audience sizes without live platform data.

# Meta Ads — Placements
Platform: meta_ads
Knowledge version: 2026-07
Source: https://www.facebook.com/business/ads/meta-advantage-plus/placements

Advantage+ placements automatically distributes delivery across eligible Meta placements to find cost-effective opportunities. Meta lists surfaces such as Facebook, Instagram, Reels, Stories, Messenger and Audience Network depending on campaign eligibility.

## Rocky rule
Defaulting to automated placements can be appropriate for performance, but manual placement restrictions may be justified by creative compatibility, brand safety, legal constraints, measurement requirements or a deliberately placement-specific experiment. Rocky should state the reason when overriding automated placements.

# Google Ads — Smart Bidding
Platform: google_ads
Knowledge version: 2026-07
Sources:
- https://support.google.com/google-ads/answer/7065882?hl=en-in
- https://support.google.com/google-ads/answer/6268632?hl=en-IN
- https://support.google.com/google-ads/answer/6268637?hl=en

Smart Bidding uses Google AI to optimize for conversions or conversion value at auction time. Current core strategies include Target CPA, Target ROAS, Maximize conversions and Maximize conversion value.

Starting June 2026, Google is changing labels: "Maximize conversions with a Target CPA" is becoming "Target CPA", and "Maximize conversion value with a Target ROAS" is becoming "Target ROAS". Google states that the underlying bidding behavior does not change because of this naming transition.

Target CPA is appropriate when the advertiser values conversion volume while trying to maintain an average acquisition-cost target. Individual conversions may be above or below the target.

Target ROAS is appropriate when conversion values differ and the advertiser wants conversion value around a return constraint. Accurate conversion values are required.

Maximize conversions prioritizes conversion volume within budget. Maximize conversion value prioritizes total conversion value within budget.

## Rocky rule
Do not fabricate CPA/ROAS targets. Derive targets from actual economics/history when available. If reliable conversion tracking is absent, flag measurement setup before recommending conversion-based automation as though it were fully informed.

# Google Ads — Search Keywords and Match Logic
Platform: google_ads
Knowledge version: 2026-07
Sources:
- https://support.google.com/google-ads/answer/14996023?hl=en
- https://support.google.com/google-ads/answer/16668865?hl=en

Google Search keyword matching includes broad, phrase and exact match. The match types overlap: phrase can reach the queries exact reaches plus more; broad can reach phrase/exact queries plus more. Modern matching considers meaning, not only literal syntax.

Broad match can use additional account/context signals. It can be useful when paired with conversion measurement and Smart Bidding, but Rocky must not blindly recommend broad match when conversion tracking is absent or the advertiser cannot tolerate exploratory traffic.

Negative keywords prevent ads from serving for unwanted queries. Negative matching behaves differently from positive keyword matching and should be used intentionally.

For Performance Max, Google recommends using negative keywords mainly for essential brand-safety or completely irrelevant traffic; brand exclusions are preferred when the goal is excluding brand searches.

## Rocky keyword workflow
1. Identify commercial intent and the offer.
2. Build tightly relevant keyword themes.
3. Separate materially different intent into logical groups.
4. Add negatives for clearly irrelevant intent.
5. Do not invent search volume, CPC, competition or forecast numbers unless Rocky has live Keyword Planner/API data.
6. Treat match type as a control/coverage decision, not a quality score.

# Google Ads — Performance Max
Platform: google_ads
Knowledge version: 2026-07
Source: https://support.google.com/google-ads/answer/10724817?hl=en-in

Performance Max is a goal-based campaign type that can access Google Ads inventory across channels. It optimizes toward specified conversion goals and uses Google AI across bidding, budgets, audiences, creatives and attribution.

Inputs can include advertising objectives, CPA/ROAS targets, creative assets, audience signals and optional data feeds.

## Rocky decision rules
- Prefer PMax when cross-channel performance toward measurable conversion goals is appropriate.
- Do not treat audience signals as hard targeting in the same sense as a manually restricted audience.
- Do not create fake performance forecasts.
- Ensure conversion goals represent valuable business outcomes rather than low-value events that could misdirect optimization.
- For ecommerce, product/feed quality is strategically important where Merchant Center feeds are involved.
- For lead generation, lead quality and offline/qualified-lead feedback should be considered where available.

# Google Ads — Campaign Type Selection
Platform: google_ads
Knowledge version: 2026-07
Source: https://support.google.com/google-ads/answer/2567043?hl=en

Google Ads campaign type should be chosen from the advertiser's actual business goal, conversion goal, creative/inventory needs, and available measurement.

## Search
Use Search when the business needs to capture existing intent from people actively searching for products or services. It is especially suitable for sales, leads and qualified website traffic. Search should not be chosen merely because the advertiser wants "more reach"; it is primarily an intent-capture channel.

## Performance Max
Performance Max is goal-based and can access Google Ads inventory across channels from one campaign. It uses Smart Bidding and Google AI across bidding, budget optimization, audiences, creatives and attribution. It should be grounded in meaningful conversion goals. Google exposes Performance Max under objectives such as Sales, Leads and Local store visits/promotions.

## Video
Video campaigns use YouTube and other eligible inventory. Choose awareness/view-oriented configurations for reach or consideration; conversion-oriented configurations should have appropriate conversion measurement.

## App
Use App campaigns when the primary business outcome concerns app installs, app engagement or in-app actions.

## Demand Gen
Demand Gen is designed for demand creation and action across visual/discovery surfaces. See the dedicated Demand Gen knowledge file.

## Rocky rule
Never select a campaign type solely from the business industry. First infer: objective → conversion action → customer intent → available assets/data → campaign type.

# Google Ads — Demand Gen
Platform: google_ads
Knowledge version: 2026-07
Source: https://support.google.com/google-ads/answer/13695777?hl=en

Demand Gen captures engagement and action across Google's visual/discovery inventory including YouTube (including Shorts), Discover, Gmail, Maps and the Google Display Network where available.

In June 2026 Google began allowing eligible advertisers to voluntarily migrate existing Display campaigns using its migration tooling, with broader migration changes planned.

Demand Gen supports goals around clicks, conversions and conversion value depending on setup. Channel controls can provide placement choices across eligible surfaces.

## Rocky rule
Use Demand Gen when the strategy is demand creation/discovery with suitable visual creative and audience strategy. Do not substitute it automatically for Search when the key opportunity is explicit high-intent search demand.

# Google Ads — India Billing and Policy Guardrails
Platform: google_ads
Country: IN
Knowledge version: 2026-07
Sources:
- https://support.google.com/google-ads/answer/2375370?hl=en
- https://support.google.com/adspolicy/answer/6020956?hl=en
- https://support.google.com/adspolicy/answer/6242605?hl=en

Google's tax documentation currently states that eligible India advertising purchases are subject to GST rules. For services from Google India Private Limited, documented rates include CGST 9% + SGST 9% for applicable intra-state treatment, or IGST 18% for applicable inter-state treatment. Google also documents separate treatment depending on the service entity and advertiser status.

Do not use this knowledge as tax advice. Rocky should surface tax information as an operational consideration and direct the business to its accountant/tax adviser for decisions.

Google advertising policies restrict misuse of user data and impose additional rules for personalized advertising, remarketing and sensitive categories.

## Rocky policy gate
Before recommending targeting, check whether the offer belongs to a regulated/sensitive category. Never recommend evasion of platform policy, identity verification, restricted targeting, disclosures or legal requirements.

# Google Ads — Conversion Measurement
Platform: google_ads
Knowledge version: 2026-07
Sources:
- https://support.google.com/google-ads/answer/9888656?hl=en-EN
- https://support.google.com/google-ads/answer/10000067?hl=en

Enhanced conversions can improve conversion measurement by supplementing existing measurement with hashed first-party customer data. Google documents SHA-256 hashing for eligible first-party fields before transmission.

Consent Mode communicates user consent choices to Google so supported tags adapt their behavior. It is not itself a consent banner; it works with the site's consent mechanism.

## Rocky measurement gate
Before producing a performance campaign plan, check:
- primary conversion actions
- whether conversion values are available and meaningful
- tag/measurement status
- enhanced conversions eligibility/setup
- consent requirements applicable to the business and users
- duplicate or low-value conversion actions
- offline/qualified lead import opportunities

Rocky must distinguish "recommended setup" from "verified setup". Never claim tracking is installed or healthy without account/site evidence.

# Rocky RAG Retrieval Instructions

Retrieve official platform knowledge before drafting a campaign plan.

Suggested metadata filters:
platform: google_ads | meta_ads | cross_platform
country: global | IN
topic: campaign_type | keywords | bidding | audience | placements | measurement | policy | budget | strategy
knowledge_version: 2026-07

For every campaign-plan generation:
1. Retrieve campaign/objective knowledge.
2. Retrieve bidding/optimization knowledge.
3. Retrieve targeting knowledge.
4. Retrieve measurement knowledge.
5. Retrieve policy/country knowledge if applicable.
6. Retrieve strategy decision rules.
7. Generate plan only after checking missing required inputs.

Prefer current official-platform chunks over older chunks when rules conflict. Preserve source_url and retrieved_at metadata for traceability.

# Rocky — Cross-Platform Campaign Decision Engine
Knowledge version: 2026-07

This file is authored strategy logic grounded by the official-platform knowledge in this pack. It is not a promise of results.

## Required inputs
business_model, offer, geography, objective, funnel_stage, budget, conversion_action, tracking_status, historical_performance, creative_assets, landing_page, sales_cycle, average_order_or_lead_value, target economics.

## Decision order
1. Validate the business goal.
2. Identify the conversion action that actually represents value.
3. Validate measurement readiness.
4. Determine demand state: capture existing intent vs create demand vs retarget/nurture.
5. Choose platform/campaign type.
6. Choose bidding/optimization compatible with measurement and economics.
7. Build audience/keyword structure.
8. Select placements/inventory.
9. Build creative/message requirements.
10. Set budget/test logic.
11. Add policy and privacy checks.
12. Define KPIs and stop/iterate criteria.

## Hard anti-hallucination rules
Rocky must never invent:
- keyword search volume
- CPC/CPM/CPA
- audience size
- conversion rate
- ROAS
- competitor spend
- platform forecasts
- "guaranteed" results
unless these values come from connected live data or explicitly supplied historical data.

Label outputs as:
VERIFIED_DATA, USER_PROVIDED_DATA, OFFICIAL_PLATFORM_RULE, STRATEGY_INFERENCE, or MISSING_DATA.

# Rocky Knowledge Source Priority
Platform: cross_platform
Level: system
Topic: provenance

When facts conflict, use this priority:
1. Live connected platform/API capability and account state.
2. Newer official Meta/Google documentation.
3. Current official API documentation.
4. IIM Bangalore-derived durable marketing strategy.
5. Skyup account/client historical evidence for that account/context.
6. Authored strategy inference.

Never let an old RAG chunk override a newer official rule. Store source URL, verification date, knowledge version, embedding model and embedding dimensions with indexed knowledge.

# Rocky Results Feedback Loop
Platform: cross_platform
Level: system
Topic: learning_loop

For every campaign retain the original hypothesis and configuration alongside time-series results. Useful evidence can include spend, impressions, reach where available, clicks, CTR, CPC, CPM, conversions, conversion value, CPA/CPL, ROAS where meaningful, frequency where available, search terms, keyword/ad-group results, audience/ad-set results, creative/ad results, lead quality, qualified pipeline and sales outcomes.

Rocky should learn conditionally: 'X performed better than Y for this account, offer, audience, geography, period and optimization setup.' It must not promote one observation into a global best practice.

Separate raw observations from derived lessons. Keep client-level learning isolated from other clients unless Skyup explicitly builds a lawful anonymized aggregate-learning layer.

# Rocky Master Paid-Media Decision Engine
Platform: cross_platform
Level: system
Topic: decision_engine

Required reasoning sequence:
1. Business outcome and offer.
2. Customer/segment and positioning.
3. Funnel stage and demand state.
4. Valuable conversion action.
5. Measurement readiness.
6. Platform and campaign type/objective.
7. Campaign-level bidding/budget logic.
8. Ad-set/ad-group/asset-group targeting and optimization.
9. Creative/ad format and destination.
10. Policy/privacy/account eligibility.
11. Deterministic payload validation.
12. Create PAUSED.
13. Human review.
14. Read live results.
15. Compare results with the original hypothesis.
16. Store structured learnings without turning one campaign result into a universal rule.

Documents define platform truth and strategy. Live account data determines account-specific truth.

# Google Smart Bidding — 2026 Naming and Behavior
Platform: google_ads
Level: campaign
Topic: smart_bidding
Source: https://support.google.com/google-ads/answer/7065882?hl=en-in
Verified: 2026-07-24

Google documents Target CPA, Target ROAS, Maximize conversions and Maximize conversion value as Smart Bidding strategies using auction-time optimization. Starting June 2026, labels are changing so 'Maximize conversions with a Target CPA' becomes 'Target CPA' and the analogous conversion-value strategy becomes 'Target ROAS'; Google states the underlying behavior is unchanged.

Rocky should normalize old/new labels internally while emitting the terminology/API enum expected by the connected platform version.

# Google Enhanced Conversions — 2026 Migration
Platform: google_ads
Level: measurement
Topic: enhanced_conversions_2026
Sources:
- https://support.google.com/google-ads/answer/14664077?hl=en
- https://support.google.com/google-ads/answer/16884284?hl=en
Verified: 2026-07-24

Google is unifying enhanced conversions for web and leads. From April 2026 Google can accept user-provided data simultaneously from website tags, Data Manager and API connections. Google states that from June 15, 2026, offline-conversion import and enhanced-conversions-for-leads uploads are migrated to Data Manager API and blocked in Google Ads API except documented legacy circumstances.

Rocky's integration code must not be designed around an obsolete upload path. Measurement implementation should follow the current Data Manager/API documentation.

# Google Performance Max — Asset Group API
Platform: google_ads
Level: asset_group
Topic: asset_group_api
Source: https://developers.google.com/google-ads/api/reference/rpc/v24/AssetGroup
Verified: 2026-07-24

An AssetGroup belongs to a campaign and links assets through AssetGroupAsset and signals through AssetGroupSignal. Current API fields include name, final URLs, mobile URLs, path fields, status, ad strength, asset coverage and primary-status diagnostics.

Rocky should use asset-group diagnostics as live account evidence after creation. Static RAG cannot predict ad strength or asset coverage.

# Google Search — Responsive Search Ad Exact Specs
Platform: google_ads
Level: ad
Topic: rsa_specs
Source: https://support.google.com/google-ads/answer/17092074?hl=en
Verified: 2026-07-24

Current official Search campaign specifications list Responsive Search Ads with headlines up to 30 characters, 1–15 headline assets, descriptions up to 90 characters, 1–4 description assets, and a final URL up to 2,048 characters.

Rocky must validate generated RSA text against these limits before submission. Character validation belongs in deterministic code as well as RAG guidance.

# Google Search — Search Asset Specifications
Platform: google_ads
Level: ad
Topic: search_asset_specs
Source: https://support.google.com/google-ads/answer/17092074?hl=en
Verified: 2026-07-24

Google's current Search specifications document business information and ad assets in addition to RSA text. Current documented examples include a business name up to 25 characters and a square logo recommendation of 1200×1200 pixels with a documented minimum of 128×128. Image assets include square 1:1 and horizontal 1.91:1 formats with current documented size guidance.

Rocky should use exact current specs only for the asset type being created and should validate uploaded files before API creation.

# Google Ads — Ad Group Execution
Platform: google_ads
Level: ad_group
Topic: api_execution
Sources:
- https://developers.google.com/google-ads/api/fields/v22/ad_group
- https://developers.google.com/google-ads/api/reference/rpc/v22/AdGroup
Verified: 2026-07-24

Ad groups expose status, type, targeting-related settings, URL/tracking settings and bidding fields. Some bidding fields only apply under compatible campaign bidding strategies.

Rocky must build the campaign first conceptually, then apply only ad-group fields compatible with that campaign's type and bidding strategy. Unsupported fields should fail preflight rather than be silently ignored by Rocky.

# Google Ads API — Criteria Compatibility Matrix
Platform: google_ads
Level: campaign_ad_group
Topic: criteria
Source: https://developers.google.com/google-ads/api/docs/targeting/criteria?hl=en
Verified: 2026-07-24

Current Google Ads API documentation distinguishes allowed levels and positive/negative support by criterion. Examples include ad schedule at campaign level; age and gender at campaign/ad-group levels; audiences at ad-group level in applicable contexts; campaign-level negative keywords; language at campaign/ad-group levels; proximity at campaign level; and user lists at campaign/ad-group levels where supported.

Rocky must query/validate the exact criterion compatibility for the selected campaign type before mutation. Never generate an API payload merely because a targeting concept exists in marketing strategy.

# Google Ads API — Targeting Levels
Platform: google_ads
Level: campaign_ad_group
Topic: targeting_api
Source: https://developers.google.com/google-ads/api/docs/targeting/overview
Verified: 2026-07-24

Google Ads API targeting can operate at customer, campaign and ad-group levels depending on criterion type. Criteria may support targeting, exclusion, bid changes, or only a subset of those operations.

Rocky must not assume every criterion is valid at every level. The connected API version is the final authority.

# Meta — Messaging / WhatsApp Campaign Rule
Platform: meta_ads
Level: ad_set_ad
Topic: messaging

When the business goal is conversations or lead qualification through messaging, Rocky should treat the messaging destination and downstream qualification flow as part of the conversion architecture, not merely as a CTA choice.

Before execution, verify the connected business assets, eligible messaging destination, current objective/performance-goal compatibility, permissions and tracking capabilities through Meta. Never assume WhatsApp is available for every ad account/page configuration.

# Meta — Live Capability Rule
Platform: meta_ads
Level: system
Topic: live_validation

Meta RAG provides objective, audience, placement, measurement and creative reasoning, but exact IDs, available optimization goals, destination combinations, account eligibility, placements and API fields must be resolved from the connected Marketing API/account.

Rocky must never convert a human-readable targeting idea into an executable Meta ID unless it came from a live supported lookup or verified stored mapping.
